body.title: Consequence check: Igor request model — no more direct claiming
body.result: outcome: clear. LegacyDirectClaimError appeared 2026-05-21 as predicted (old engrams hitting new path); no instances today (2026-05-22 ~11:00 UTC+). Igor has active in_progress tickets. Transition landed cleanly — old code path surfaced and resolved. No stuck loop observed.
body.description:
**What changed:** D-igor-request-not-claim-2026-05-20 — adopt_next_ticket migrated to cmd_next, LegacyDirectClaimError added for old code paths.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) Old engrams (SKILL_SP, SKILL_SPRINT_ENTRY) still describe the direct-claim model — they may mislead Igor's cognition into attempting the old path, which will now raise rather than fail silently. (2) If cmd_next returns None more often than expected (e.g., all sprint tickets are difficulty>1), Igor may appear 'stuck' when he's actually working correctly — this needs to be distinguished from the previous broken-stuck state. (3) LegacyDirectClaimError in logs may fire during the transition window before old engrams are updated.
**Signals to watch:** Channel: `LegacyDirectClaimError` log lines indicate old code path hits (good — surfaces location). Absence of `[pe_chain] ✗ T-test-ticket` after 72h. Igor's NE cycles producing results again (not stuck in empty-queue loop).
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-03 or 72 hours after T-adopt-next-uses-cmd-next closes.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome and close
**Test plan:** no tests — observation ticket
